title: Base schema macro with tenant_id, timestamps, and soft delete support

story.i_want_to: define a base schema macro that automatically includes tenant_id, inserted_at/updated_at timestamps, and optional soft delete support (deleted_at field with query filtering)
story.so_that: every schema in the system has consistent multi-tenant scoping, timestamp tracking, and soft delete behavior without duplicating boilerplate across dozens of modules

rationale: loopctl is multi-tenant from day one and every table must include tenant_id for RLS enforcement. A base schema macro ensures no developer (human or AI) can accidentally create a schema without tenant scoping. Soft delete via deleted_at preserves audit trails while allowing logical removal. Centralizing this in a macro eliminates inconsistency risk across 15+ schemas.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Schema module exists that provides a `use Loopctl.Schema` macro for Ecto schemas
The macro sets @primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true} and @foreign_key_type :binary_id on all schemas that use it

The base schema macro provides a `tenant_field()` macro that developers MUST call inside their `schema` block to add the tenant_id belongs_to association. The macro CANNOT inject fields automatically via `__using__/1` because Ecto requires fields inside the `schema` block. Usage: `schema "my_table" do; tenant_field(); field :name, :string; timestamps(); end`. The tenant_field() macro expands to `belongs_to :tenant, Loopctl.Tenants.Tenant, type: :binary_id` with appropriate foreign key configuration.
The macro sets @timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec] for microsecond-precision UTC timestamps
When the soft_delete: true option is passed, the macro adds a deleted_at field of type :utc_datetime_usec defaulting to nil
A Loopctl.Schema.soft_delete_changeset/1 function marks a record as deleted by setting deleted_at to the current UTC time
A Loopctl.Schema.not_deleted/1 query helper filters out records where deleted_at is not nil
Schemas can opt out of tenant_id by passing tenant_scoped: false (for the tenants table itself and superadmin-only tables)
The macro is documented with @moduledoc explaining usage patterns and options

test_cases.name: Schema using base macro has binary_id primary key
preconditions: A test schema module uses Loopctl.Schema
steps: Define a test schema with `use Loopctl.Schema`

test_cases.name: Tenant-scoped schema requires tenant_id in changeset
preconditions: A test schema with use Loopctl.Schema is defined
expected_results: Changeset is invalid with error on tenant_id: "can't be blank"

test_cases.name: Soft delete sets deleted_at timestamp
preconditions: A test schema with use Loopctl.Schema, soft_delete: true is defined
expected_results: The changeset sets deleted_at to a non-nil UTC datetime
The deleted_at value is close to DateTime.utc_now()

test_cases.name: not_deleted/1 query excludes soft-deleted records
expected_results: Only the non-deleted record is returned

test_cases.name: Timestamps use utc_datetime_usec
preconditions: A test schema with use Loopctl.Schema is defined
expected_results: Both fields are DateTime structs with microsecond precision
Both fields are in UTC timezone

test_cases.name: Non-tenant-scoped schema omits tenant_id
preconditions: A test schema with use Loopctl.Schema, tenant_scoped: false is defined
expected_results: tenant_id is not in the list of fields

test_cases.name: Schema without soft_delete option has no deleted_at field
preconditions: A test schema with use Loopctl.Schema (no soft_delete option) is defined
expected_results: deleted_at is not in the list of fields

test_cases.name: Cross-tenant isolation: tenant A cannot access tenant B data
expected_results: Returns empty list or 404, never tenant_a's data

technical_notes: The base schema macro CANNOT inject fields directly via __using__/1 because Ecto requires fields inside the schema block. Two implementation approaches: (1) Provide a tenant_field() macro that developers call inside their schema block: `schema "my_table" do; tenant_field(); field :name, :string; end`. (2) Use @before_compile to modify module attributes. Approach 1 is recommended — it's explicit and follows Ecto conventions.
Use __using__/1 macro with keyword options: tenant_scoped (default true), soft_delete (default false)
tenant_id should be belongs_to :tenant, Loopctl.Tenants.Tenant, type: :binary_id — but the Tenant schema may not exist yet; use a plain field for now and add the association later
Consider providing a base_changeset/2 function that validates tenant_id presence, which downstream schemas can pipe into
The not_deleted/1 helper should use Ecto.Query: where(query, [r], is_nil(r.deleted_at))
Ensure the macro works with both schema/2 and embedded_schema/1 patterns if needed

Testing infrastructure: (1) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (2) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup via Mox.stub/3, (3) Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert + auto-deps), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
